P2_fixed_point_test.verdict: psi*(A) and psi*(B) do NOT converge to a common field under extra iteration (distance does not -> 0), so there is no seed-independent attractor to mass-produce collisions.

P3_serialization_aliasing.note: a5 shows one logical field has multiple byte images. These are encoding faults (same input, two commitments), the OPPOSITE of a 2nd-preimage (two inputs, one commitment). Not a collision against the stated claim.

P4_birthday_bound.verdict: No PDE shortcut to collisions observed; second-preimage resistance is inherited from SHA-256, not added by WaveLock.

OVERALL: No practical second preimage found. The PDE neither collapses to a shared attractor nor exhibits sub-birthday structure; collision/2nd-preimage resistance rests entirely on SHA-256.
